changes: OpenClaw shipped fresh stable release v2026.4.27, published on 2026-04-29 22:12 UTC.
nanobot shipped v0.1.5.post3 on 2026-04-29 10:57 UTC, extending the lightweight lane's fast release cadence.
Hermes Agent still has no newer release than v2026.4.23, but it climbed again to roughly 125.7k stars in this scan.
SwarmClaw also shipped v1.7.2 on 2026-04-29 22:15 UTC.
The rest of the Tier 1 and Tier 2 map stayed active, but there is no material taxonomy shift versus the previous run.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw shipping again matters most because the category anchor is still setting pace rather than just defending share.
nanobot's continued release cadence reinforces that lightweight rewrites remain the busiest direct derivative pressure.
Hermes' continued growth reinforces that the learning-loop lane remains strategically important even without a newer tag.
SwarmClaw shipping alongside OpenClaw reinforces that orchestration and control-plane layers are getting more real, even if the durable map did not change.
Net: this run had real ecosystem motion, but it still reinforces the existing strategic read rather than changing it.
